title: Monetary Policy Report—January 2026
summary: The Bank of Canada released its January 2026 Monetary Policy Report maintaining the overnight policy rate at 2.25%, with the Bank Rate at 2.5% and deposit rate at 2.20%. US tariffs and trade uncertainty continue to disrupt the Canadian economy, with growth projected to average around 1¼% over the projection horizon while inflation remains near the 2% target.
key_points: Bank of Canada holds target overnight rate at 2.25%, with Bank Rate at 2.5% and deposit rate at 2.20%
US tariffs remain elevated and the future of North American trade continues to be highly uncertain
Canadian economic growth expected to average around 1¼% over the projection horizon
CPI inflation remains near the 2% target as excess supply offsets upward cost pressures
Canadian businesses are expanding exports to countries other than the United States and reducing reliance on US imports
The Canada–United States–Mexico Agreement is under review with wide-ranging potential outcomes and impacts
why_it_matters: This quarterly Monetary Policy Report establishes the Bank of Canada's baseline outlook for the Canadian economy. With the policy rate on hold at 2.25%, the central bank signals it sees current policy as appropriate despite trade disruptions. The unusually high risks cited due to geopolitical uncertainty and trade agreement review provide context for potential future policy adjustments. Canadian dollar and domestic rate sensitivity make this essential reading for CAD traders and anyone with Canadian equity or bond exposure.
macro_context: The report presents a nuanced macro picture: modest growth around 1¼% but inflation near target. US trade restrictions are causing structural adjustments that will take time to unfold. Global growth is expected to remain solid, supported by AI investment boom and fiscal stimulus in major economies. The balance of excess supply against upward cost pressures keeps inflation anchored near 2%.
